skill.name: convex-quickstart
skill.description: Initializes a new Convex project from scratch or adds Convex to an existing app. Use this skill when starting a new project with Convex, scaffolding with npm create convex@latest, adding Convex to an existing React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, or other frontend, wiring up ConvexProvider, configuring environment variables for the deployment URL, or running npx convex dev for the first time, even if the user just says "set up Convex" or "add a backend."
skill.summary: Scaffold a new Convex backend or integrate Convex into an existing frontend project with proper configuration and setup.
skill.author: get-convex

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 125 static findings across 349 lines in 2 files. All major findings are false positives or expected patterns for a legitimate scaffolding tool. The skill contains npm scaffolding commands (expected), references .env.local for configuration (standard Convex setup), and documentation URLs. No malicious intent or credential exfiltration patterns detected.

risk_factor_evidence.justification: FALSE POSITIVE. The scanner flagged backtick characters in markdown code blocks and example commands as shell execution. All detected locations are documentation examples (npm create, npx convex dev, npm install) rather than actual command execution calls.
FALSE POSITIVE. The path '../convex/_generated/api' is standard relative import syntax in example React component code, not a path traversal attack.

EXPECTED_PATTERN. References to .env.local and environment variable names (CONVEX_URL, VITE_CONVEX_URL) are standard Convex configuration patterns. The skill describes how Convex writes its deployment URL to .env.local during setup - this is legitimate configuration, not credential access.

FALSE POSITIVE. Network references are localhost development URLs (localhost:5173, localhost:3000) and official Convex documentation links (docs.convex.dev). No external data exfiltration or malicious network calls detected.

content.user_title: Set up a Convex backend in seconds
content.value_statement: Getting started with Convex backend development requires multiple setup steps including scaffolding, provider configuration, and environment setup. This skill automates the project setup process for new and existing applications.
actual_capabilities: Scaffold a new Convex project using official templates (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte)
Integrate Convex into an existing frontend application with proper provider setup
Configure environment variables for different frameworks (Vite, Next.js, Remix, React Native)
Set up anonymous agent mode for headless or cloud environments
Verify Convex setup by checking generated types and deployment URL configuration
limitations: Cannot run interactive commands like npx convex dev (requires user browser login)
Does not set up authentication - use convex-setup-auth skill for that
Does not handle existing Convex installations - user should just start building
Cannot deploy to production - this skill focuses on local development setup

use_cases.title: Start a new fullstack project with Convex
use_cases.description: Quickly scaffold a complete Convex project with React, Next.js, or other frontend frameworks using official templates with all configuration wired together.
use_cases.target_user: Developers starting a new project who want Convex as their backend

use_cases.title: Add Convex backend to an existing app
use_cases.description: Integrate Convex into a React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, or other existing frontend by installing the package, configuring the provider, and setting up environment variables.
use_cases.target_user: Developers with existing projects who want to add real-time backend capabilities

use_cases.title: Set up Convex in CI or headless environments
use_cases.description: Configure Convex for cloud agents or continuous integration using anonymous mode that avoids interactive browser authentication.
use_cases.target_user: Developers working with AI coding agents or automated build systems

prompt_templates.title: New Convex project from scratch
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a new Convex project. I want to use React with Vite and Tailwind CSS.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to start a brand new project with Convex as the backend

prompt_templates.title: Add Convex to existing Next.js app
prompt_templates.prompt: Add Convex backend to my existing Next.js application. I've already run npm install.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has an existing Next.js project and wants to integrate Convex

prompt_templates.title: Quick Convex setup for prototyping
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a working Convex backend as fast as possible for a prototype. Use the bare template.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants minimal setup for rapid prototyping

prompt_templates.title: Set up Convex with shadcn/ui
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Convex project with Next.js, shadcn/ui components, and Clerk authentication pre-configured.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a complete stack with UI components and auth

output_examples.input: Set up Convex for a new React project with the bare template
output_examples.output: Project scaffolded successfully. Run 'npm install' in the my-app directory, then run 'npx convex dev' to start. The Convex deployment URL will be written to .env.local automatically. Created convex/schema.ts and convex/_generated/ types.

output_examples.input: Add Convex to my existing Vite React app
output_examples.output: Installed convex package. Created ConvexProvider wrapper with ConvexReactClient using VITE_CONVEX_URL. Add CONVEX_URL=your-deployment-url to your .env.local file. Run 'npx convex dev' to connect to a deployment.

best_practices: Let users run 'npx convex dev' themselves to handle browser-based OAuth authentication
Use the react-vite-shadcn template as the default for new projects unless specified otherwise
Create ConvexReactClient at module scope, not inside components, to avoid re-creating clients on every render
anti_patterns: Do not scaffold inside an existing directory with files - it may overwrite existing code
Do not run 'npx convex dev' directly in an agent - it requires interactive browser login
Do not skip the npm install step - the scaffolding tool creates files but does not install dependencies

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, React Native, TanStack Start, Remix, and Node.js. Templates are available for React and Next.js with optional auth providers like Clerk and Lucia.

faq.question: Why should users run 'npx convex dev' themselves?
faq.answer: The convex dev command requires browser-based OAuth authentication on first run, which cloud and headless agents cannot complete. The skill instructs users to run this command directly.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with an existing Convex project?
faq.answer: No. If the project already has Convex installed and the convex/ directory exists, you should skip scaffolding and just start building. This skill is only for initial setup.

faq.question: What environment variables does Convex need?
faq.answer: The variable name depends on your framework: VITE_CONVEX_URL for Vite, NEXT_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL for Next.js, EXPO_PUBLIC_CONVEX_URL for React Native. The npx convex dev command writes these automatically.

faq.question: How do I set up Convex for a headless agent?
faq.answer: Set CONVEX_AGENT_MODE=anonymous in your environment or .env.local file. This allows the Convex backend to run locally without requiring browser authentication.

faq.question: What's the difference between development and production?
faq.answer: Use 'npx convex dev' for development - it syncs code on save to your personal dev deployment. Use 'npx convex deploy' when ready to ship - it pushes to your production deployment.
